body: Does the choc package `starship.portable` (or winget with the zip source) work any better (they should start starship through a shim)? Since v1.21 the executables and installer are signed which might have an impact, please also check if the unsigned versions [which are uploaded during release build](https://github.com/starship/starship/actions/runs/11407408400) avoid the issue.

This had "gone away for a while". Sorry don't know what caused the problem to stop happening.
Today it's back.

After uninstalling:
```
C:\Users\edgeb1>choco list | rg starship
starship 1.21.1
starship.install 1.21.1

C:\Users\edgeb1>choco uninstall starship
Chocolatey v2.4.1
Uninstalling the following packages:
starship
You are uninstalling starship, which is likely a metapackage for an
 *.install/*.portable package that it installed
 (starship represents discoverability).
For the question below, you have 20 seconds to make a selection.
Would you like to uninstall starship.install as well?([Y]es/[N]o): Y
```

and installing the `'portable` package:
```
C:\Users\edgeb1>choco install starship.portable
Chocolatey v2.4.1
Installing the following packages:
starship.portable
By installing, you accept licenses for the packages.
Downloading package from source 'https://community.chocolatey.org/api/v2/'
Progress: Downloading starship.portable 1.21.1... 100%

starship.portable v1.21.1 [Approved]
starship.portable package files install completed. Performing other installation steps.
Downloading starship.portable 64 bit
  from 'https://github.com/starship/starship/releases/download/v1.21.1/starship-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip'
Progress: 100% - Completed download of C:\Users\edgeb1\AppData\Local\Temp\chocolatey\starship.portable\1.21.1\starship-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (3.27 MB).
Download of starship-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip (3.27 MB) completed.
Hashes match.
Extracting C:\Users\edgeb1\AppData\Local\Temp\chocolatey\starship.portable\1.21.1\starship-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip to C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\starship.portable\tools...
C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\starship.portable\tools
Add the following to the end of ~\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1 'Invoke-Expression (&starship init powershell)'
 ShimGen has successfully created a shim for starship.exe
 The install of starship.portable was successful.
  Deployed to 'C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\starship.portable\tools'

Chocolatey installed 1/1 packages.
 See the log for details (C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\logs\chocolatey.log).
```
Now I get:
```
Unhandled Exception: System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: This program is blocked by group policy. For more information, contact your system administrator
   at System.Diagnostics.Process.StartWithCreateProcess(ProcessStartInfo startInfo)
   at EdrDotNet.Hooks.ProcessStartHook.ProcessStartHandler(Process process)
   at shim.CommandExecutor.execute(String process, String arguments, String working_directory, Boolean is_gui, Boolean wait_for_exit, Boolean requires_elevation)
   at shim.ShimProgram.Main(String[] args)
```

It came to me that the last time this happened I was able to work around it using the `scoop` installer. This time that does not work.
I tried several of the alternate installers with similar results:
```
choco install starship
wget install starship
scoop install starship
```
All with similar results:
```
Unhandled Exception: System.ComponentModel.Win32Exception: This program is blocked by group policy. For more information, contact your system administrator
   at System.Diagnostics.Process.StartWithCreateProcess(ProcessStartInfo startInfo)
   at EdrDotNet.Hooks.ProcessStartHook.ProcessStartHandler(Process process)
   at shim.CommandExecutor.execute(String process, String arguments, String working_directory, Boolean is_gui, Boolean wait_for_exit, Boolean requires_elevation)
   at shim.ShimProgram.Main(String[] args)
CAPT=/c/Users/edgeb1/git/sbom/capt/cli/target
```
or simply
```
bash: /c/Users/edgeb1/scoop/apps/starship/current/starship.exe: Permission denied
```

This happened to me on a domain connected computer. See error in the following (Event Viewer → Applications and Services Logs → Microsoft → Windows → CodeIntegrity → Operational)

```
Code Integrity determined that a process (\Device\HarddiskVolume4\Program Files\PowerShell\7\pwsh.exe) attempted to load \Device\HarddiskVolume4\Program Files\starship\bin\starship.exe that did not meet the Enterprise signing level requirements or violated code integrity policy (Policy ID:{33a59c90-229d-41cf-8295-5afede1e9352}).
```

It seems that the devs would have to sign the executable to resolve this error (at least for me).
Looking at the exe properties, it seems to already be signed by a Digikey cert. I'm not sure what exactly is triggering this policy violation.
